query: autosomal dominant hypercholesterolemia type II causes

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Genetic disorders with high LDL-C are most commonly caused by autosomal dominant conditions and rarely inherited as a recessive trait. Mutations in the LDL receptor (LDLR; “classic FH”) cause 80% to 90% of cases of clinical FH and have an autosomal dominant inheritance. Rarely, mutations in APOB and PCSK9 can cause autosomal dominant hypercholesterolemia. However, the clinical phenotype cannot distinguish between these three mutations. [1] APOB synthesizes the major protein in LDL, which is the ligand for LDLR. A mutation resulting in defective ApoB-100 results in impaired binding of LDL particles to LDLR and thus impaired clearance. PCSK9 synthesizes a circulatory protein that limits LDL receptor lifespan. Therefore, gain-of-function mutation results in FH. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: The normal number and function of LDL receptors in the liver are important for the uptake of LDL cholesterol. Receptor-negative FH patients have less than 2%, and receptor-defective patients have about 2% to 25% of normal LDLR activity. There are five different classes of alleles based on the phenotypic behavior of mutant protein that results from mutations at the LDLR locus. These are as follows: Class I: Null, synthesis of LDLR is defective Class II: Transport defective, intracellular transport from the endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi apparatus is impaired. Class III: Binding defective, binding of LDL protein to the receptor is defective. Class IV: Internalization defective, proteins can bind normally, but receptors do not internalize LDL proteins do too ineffective clustering in the coated pits Class V: Defective recycling, dissociation of the receptor and like and is interrupted, thereby interrupting recycling of the receptor. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Familial hypercholesterolemia (FH) is a group of inherited genetic defects that lead to the severe elevation of serum cholesterol concentrations. Clinically familial hypercholesterolemia is diagnosed by a high serum level of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and genetically is characterized into two subgroups: (1) autosomal dominant (AD), (2) codominant transmission with 90% or higher penetrance. [1] A dominant trait transmission is the most common type of familial hypercholesterolemia. In the Fredrickson classification, patients with familial hypercholesterolemia have been seen in type 2a, 2b, and 3 hyperlipidemias; however, type 2a is the most common familial hypercholesterolemia type. [2] Elevation of serum LDL cholesterol in patients with familial hypercholesterolemia leads to an increase in the risk of atherosclerotic disease and, subsequently, premature death. [3] Early detection of familial hypercholesterolemia and aggressive management to lower the LDL cholesterol level helps in preventing or slowing the progression of coronary atherosclerosis. First-degree relatives of a patient with FH should be screened, so that other gene carriers can be identified and treated. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Homozygous FH patients often have untreated LDL-C levels above 500 mg/dL, but lower levels do not rule out HoFH. HoFH causes severe CAD by the first and second decade of life. Heterozygotes are also at increased risk of CAD between 30 to 60 years of age. Stroke is uncommon. Xanthomas, which are yellowish patches of cholesterol build-up due to extremely high levels of LDL-C, can occur around eyelids (xanthelasma), within tendons of the elbows, hands, knees, and feet. A thickened Achilles tendon should raise a high level of suspicion for HoFH. The prevalence of xanthomas increases with age. Another diagnostic sign on the physical exam is corneal arcus which is a white, gray, or blue opaque ring around the corneal margin when seen in individuals younger than 45 years of age. [8] [6] [9] [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Familial hypercholesterolemia (FH) is an autosomal dominant disorder of lipid metabolism. It is characterized by very high low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) levels due to a gene mutation causing premature coronary heart disease. Homozygous individuals typically acquired disease in childhood. The majority of cases are undiagnosed and sub-optimally treated. Early diagnosis and appropriate treatment are essential in preventing premature death. [1] [2] [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Over 1600 mutations in the LDLR causing classic FH have been identified. Mutations can be homozygous or heterozygous. True homozygotes have the same mutation in both alleles, whereas compound heterozygotes have different mutations in each allele. Compound heterozygotes have a similar phenotype to homozygous FH. Patients with less than 2% of LDLR activity are classified as receptor-negative, and patients with two to 5% of regular LDLR activity are classified as receptor defective.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: It is important to note that about 30 to 50% of patients with FH phenotype do not have an identifiable gene defect. Thus, gene testing is not a requisite for the diagnosis, which is, in most cases, clinical.FH caused by a de novo mutation is very rare, and therefore, documenting detailed family history is very important. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Familial hypercholesterolemia is a group of inherited genetic defects that lead to the severe elevation of serum cholesterol concentrations. Clinically familial hypercholesterolemia is diagnosed by a high serum level of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and genetically is characterized into two subgroups: (1) autosomal dominant (AD), (2) codominant transmission with 90% or higher penetrance. This activity reviews the evaluation and management of familial hypercholesterolemia and highlights the interprofessional team's role in evaluating and treating patients with this disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of patients with familial hypercholesterolemia is worse than those without this disease at any level of untreated low-density lipoprotein cholesterol. Patients with acute coronary syndrome and a history of heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia treated with high dose statins have three times the highest risk of death and nonfatal myocardial infarction within one year when compared to matched individuals without familial hypercholesterolemia.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Familial hypercholesterolemia is a disorder that results from absent or defective low-density lipid-protein (LDL) receptors. The LDLR gene is located on the short arm of chromosome 19, and the LDLR is comprised of 860 amino acids. The LDLR is primarily responsible for hepatic uptake of LDL, which constitutes about 70% of circulating LDL. Two ligands, apolipoprotein B-100, and apo-E bind to the receptor.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Cascade screening is a cost-effective, evidence-based intervention, starting with first-degree relatives of patients with FH by cholesterol screening with or without genetic testing. The use of genetic testing in cascade screening improves the detection rate of FH. Cascade screening can lead to early diagnosis and institution of therapy for FH resulting in ASCVD reduction. National and international organizations have recommended cascade screening for FH. Concerns regarding privacy require that the patient with FH make the first contact with family members. For patients diagnosed with FH, the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act protects against health insurance and employment discrimination.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: It is important to exclude secondary causes of hypercholesterolemia such as hypothyroidism, nephrotic syndrome, and liver disease by history and laboratory testing. Diagnosis of FH in most cases is: Clinical , by a personal history of early CAD, family history of premature CAD (definite MI or sudden death, at an age younger than 55 in male first-degree relative or younger than 65 in a first-degree female relative) Physical exam findings (tendon xanthomas and premature corneal arcus) Elevated lipid levels (LDL-C greater than 190 mg/dL)

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Heterozygous FH (HeFH) is the most common monogenic, autosomal dominant disorder in humans. It occurs in approximately one in 300  people. Homozygous FH (HoFH) has a prevalence of one in 160,000 to 500,000 people. The prevalence is 18 times higher in patients with atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and almost 21 times higher in patients with premature ischemic heart disease. The prevalence of FH is higher in Dutch Afrikaners, French Canadians, Ashkenazi Jews, Christian Lebanese, and some Tunisian groups. There is a “gene dosing effect,” and homozygotes are more severely affected than heterozygotes. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Click here for a simplified version. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Reduced or absent uptake of LDL cholesterol by the liver leads to very high LDL cholesterol levels from birth with resultant premature atherosclerosis, critical aortic stenosis, and coronary artery disease (CAD).

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Hypercholesterolemia, Type 2A(Archived) -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: In the differential diagnosis of hypercholesterolemia and premature atherosclerosis, familial combined hyperlipidemia, hyperapobetalipoproteinemia, familial dysbetalipoproteinemia, and polygenic hypercholesterolemia should be considered. [15] The other causes of tendon xanthomas and premature atherosclerosis include rare genetic disorders such as sitosterolemia and cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1214
retrieved_docs.content: Autosomal dominant inheritance is the mode of transmission for فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ (hyperlipidemia type IIa). This genetic pattern means that a single copy of the altered gene inherited from either parent is sufficient to cause the disorder. فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ is characterized by markedly elevated low‑density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels, which increase the risk of premature atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease. Because the condition follows an autosomal dominant pattern, it often appears in successive generations, and first‑degree relatives of an affected individual have a 50 % chance of inheriting the same lipid abnormality. Recognizing the autosomal dominant inheritance of فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ is essential for family screening, early diagnosis, and implementation of lipid‑lowering therapies to reduce cardiovascular risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1975
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism encompass conditions such as فرط البروتينات الشحمية (hyperlipoproteinemia). Within this broader category, فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ (type IIa hyperlipidemia) is a specific manifestation that is mapped to the class of lipid‑metabolism disorders. Type IIa hyperlipidemia follows an Autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, meaning that Autosomal dominant inheritance is the genetic transmission mechanism for this particular disorder. Recognizing that فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ is a form of فرط البروتينات الشحمية and is inherited autosomally dominant helps clinicians diagnose, screen families, and manage patients with lipid metabolism abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 3 -- Community 1608
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism are classified as a form of metabolic disorder (اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي) and include hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية). This category is mapped to type IIa hyperlipidemia (فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ), a condition that follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern (Autosomal dominant inheritance). Recognizing that type IIa hyperlipidemia falls under the broader umbrella of lipid metabolism disorders and is inherited autosomally dominant helps clinicians with diagnosis, genetic counseling, and the management of patients who present with elevated blood lipids.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 2 -- Community 566
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism represent a broad category of metabolic conditions that include hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية) as a specific type of lipid disorder. Within this category, elevated blood glucose and new‑onset diabetes (ارتفاع السكر في الدم/داء السكري البادِئُ الجديد (SMQ)) are also considered members, highlighting the clinical overlap between dyslipidemia and early glucose dysregulation. The disorder group is mapped to familial hyperlipidemia type IIa (فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ), a well‑characterized lipid abnormality that follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, as indicated by the relationship between Autosomal dominant inheritance and فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ. This interconnected framework underscores how lipid metabolism disorders, specific hyperlipoproteinemia subtypes, and early diabetic manifestations are linked both pathophysiologically and genetically, informing comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 669
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism are recognized as a specific form of metabolic disorder (اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي) and are also classified under hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية). Within this category, elevated blood glucose and new‑onset diabetes (ارتفاع السكر في الدم/داء السكري البادِئُ الجديد (SMQ)) are considered member conditions, highlighting the close link between lipid abnormalities and early disturbances in carbohydrate metabolism. Laboratory results that are “above reference range” are interpreted as indicative of a disorder of lipid metabolism, underscoring the diagnostic relevance of lipid panels. The disorder “فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ” (type IIa hyperlipidemia) is mapped to the broader concept of disorders of lipid metabolism and is known to follow an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, indicating that a single copy of the pathogenic allele can transmit the condition. Moreover, carbohydrate metabolism disorders (اضطراب استقلاب الكربوهيدرات) are positioned as a parent category to metabolic disorders (اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي), reflecting the hierarchical organization of metabolic disease entities. Together, these relationships illustrate how lipid metabolism disorders intersect with broader metabolic pathways, genetic inheritance, and clinical interpretation, informing comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 529
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism represent a broad category of metabolic conditions that encompass several specific entities. Within this category, اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي (metabolic disorder) and فرط البروتينات الشحمية (hyperlipoproteinemia) are recognized as subtypes, indicating that these conditions are forms of lipid metabolism disorders. Additionally, ارتفاع السكر في الدم/داء السكري البادِئُ الجديد (SMQ) (elevated blood glucose/new‑onset diabetes) is listed as a member of disorders of lipid metabolism, highlighting the clinical overlap between dyslipidemia and early diabetes. The condition فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ (type IIa hyperlipidemia) is mapped to disorders of lipid metabolism and is known to follow an Autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, emphasizing the genetic basis of this specific lipid disorder. Furthermore, اضطراب استقلاب الكربوهيدرات (carbohydrate metabolism disorder) is identified as having اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي as its parent, linking carbohydrate metabolic abnormalities to the broader metabolic disorder framework. Together, these relationships illustrate how lipid and carbohydrate metabolic disorders are interrelated, how specific lipid abnormalities can be genetically inherited, and how they may present clinically alongside early diabetic changes, informing a comprehensive approach to patient assessment and management.

In classification systems, disorders of lipid metabolism are mapped to فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ, a form of familial hyperlipidemia. فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ follows an Autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, meaning that the trait is transmitted through a single copy of the altered gene from an affected parent. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1213
retrieved_docs.content: Xanthoma due to primary hypercholesterolemia is classified under disorders of lipid metabolism, indicating that this cutaneous lesion represents a manifestation of abnormal lipid processing associated with primary hypercholesterolemia.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 435
retrieved_docs.content: "Above reference range" serves as an interpretation used when evaluating "disorders of lipid metabolism." The category "disorders of lipid metabolism" encompasses several more specific entities. It includes the subcategory "Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën" (metabolic disorders of lipoproteins and other lipidemias), which in turn has a child concept "فرط ليبيدات الدم" (hyperlipidemia). Within the broader disorder group, the condition "فرط البروتينات الشحمية" (hyperlipoproteinemia) is recognized as a type of disorder of lipid metabolism, and the specific disorder "فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ" (type IIa hyperlipidemia) is directly mapped to the same overarching category. "فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ" follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, as indicated by the relationship with "Autosomal dominant inheritance." This hierarchical and inheritance information clarifies how various lipid metabolism disorders are related, how they are interpreted in clinical laboratory results, and the genetic considerations important for patient diagnosis and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 618
retrieved_docs.content: Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën is a subcategory within the broader classification of disorders of lipid metabolism. Within this hierarchy, فرط ليبيدات الدم (hyperlipidemia) is a child of Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën. The general group disorders of lipid metabolism is also linked to فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ (hyperlipidemia type IIa), which follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern. Clinically, Xanthoma due to primary hypercholesterolemia is mapped to disorders of lipid metabolism, illustrating a typical cutaneous manifestation of these lipid disorders. Understanding these relationships clarifies the classification, genetic basis, and clinical presentation of lipid metabolism disorders, aiding diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2759
retrieved_docs.content: The qualifier “vrozené” (meaning congenital or hereditary) is recognized as an allowed descriptor for both Alzheimer’s‑type dementia (الخرف من نوع الزهايمر) and hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية). Applying “vrozené” to Alzheimer’s‑type dementia highlights the existence of early‑onset or familial forms of the disease that arise from genetic factors, while its use with hyperlipoproteinemia denotes the inherited variants of lipid metabolism disorders. Recognizing these hereditary forms aids clinicians in genetic counseling, risk assessment, and tailoring management strategies for patients who present with a congenital basis for either neurodegenerative or metabolic conditions.

The SMQ is further characterized by the clinical effect تأثير إدرار البول, reflecting the polyuric manifestation commonly seen in new‑onset hyperglycemia. Additionally, the disorder فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ is mapped to the disorders of lipid metabolism group and is known to follow an Autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, highlighting a genetic basis for this specific hyperlipidemic condition. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 2 -- Community 517

Disorders of lipid metabolism itself is a subcategory of the Arabic‑named category فرص البروتينات الشحمية, placing it within an even wider classification of hyperlipoproteinemia. The disorder group disorders of lipid metabolism is mapped to the condition فرص شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ (hyperlipidemia type IIa), which is characterized by an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern. Moreover, Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën has a child condition called فرص ليبيدات الدم, further extending the hierarchy of related lipid disorders. This hierarchical organization clarifies how specific lipid metabolism disorders relate to broader hyperlipoproteinemic categories and highlights the genetic inheritance pattern of type IIa hyperlipidemia, aiding clinicians in diagnosis, classification, and genetic counseling.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2049
retrieved_docs.content: The term “vrozené” (congenital) is an allowed qualifier for both “فرط البروتينات الشحمية” and “انفصال الشبكية,” indicating that these conditions can be described as congenital forms. Recognizing the congenital nature of hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية) and retinal detachment (انفصال الشبكية) is important for accurate diagnosis, genetic counseling, and tailored patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1880
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism constitute a broad category of metabolic conditions affecting the processing and regulation of fats in the body, and hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية) is a specific disorder within this category. Hyperlipoproteinemia is characterized by abnormally high levels of lipoproteins in the blood, reflecting a particular manifestation of the underlying disturbances in lipid metabolism. Recognizing hyperlipoproteinemia as a subset of disorders of lipid metabolism underscores its role in the spectrum of lipid‑related diseases and informs targeted diagnostic and therapeutic approaches.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 2 -- Community 1347
retrieved_docs.content: Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën is classified as a child of disorders of lipid metabolism, indicating that it falls under the broader category of lipid metabolism disorders. Disorders of lipid metabolism is itself a subtype of اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي, the Arabic term for metabolic disorder, and is directly mapped to فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ, the condition known as type IIa hyperlipidemia. The inheritance pattern for فرط شحميات الدم من النوع الثاني أ is autosomal dominant inheritance, meaning the disorder is transmitted in an autosomal dominant manner. Within the hierarchy, Stofwisselingsstoornissen van lipoproteïnen en overige lipidemieën also encompasses فرط ليبيدات الدم as a more specific child concept, representing hyperlipidemia in general. Together, these relationships clarify that the Dutch‑named lipid‑protein metabolic disorders are part of the larger metabolic disorder spectrum, are associated with type IIa hyperlipidemia, follow an autosomal dominant inheritance, and include broader hyperlipidemia conditions.
